commit_message:
[usb/top] Remove AND gates on non-AON domain and rename 3.3V signal

Fix #7802

Signed-off-by: Michael Schaffner <msf@opentitan.org>
pull_title: [usb/top] Remove AND gates on non-AON domain and rename 3.3V signal
Issue title: [usb] USB diff rx control signals should be in AON power domain
Issue Body: https://github.com/lowRISC/opentitan/blob/17d2866a497d7bb0e891e32eddb99c943e3cbafb/hw/top_earlgrey/rtl/autogen/chip_earlgrey_asic.sv#L731    Currently the control signals are glue logic in top level which is **in main pd domain**.     it would probably be a good idea to review all other USB control signals as well.
Comments: msfschaffner: @arnonsha, it looks like this only concerns a few and gates on these USB signals.   The pinmux itself (which is driving these signals) is on AON.    Would it make sense to declare the `u_prim_usb_diff_rx` as AON and move these AND gates into that block?  https://github.com/lowRISC/opentitan/blob/17d2866a497d7bb0e891e32eddb99c943e3cbafb/hw/top_earlgrey/rtl/autogen/chip_earlgrey_asic.sv#L743-L754 tjaychen: we could also alternatively move these functions into the usb wakeup module declared inside pinmux, and directly wire that output instead of through dio/mio.  On Wed, Aug 18, 2021 at 12:16 PM Michael Schaffner ***@***.***> wrote:  > @arnonsha <https://github.com/arnonsha>, it looks like this only concerns > a few and gates. the pinmux itself (which is driving these signals) is on > aon. > > Would it make sense to declare the u_prim_usb_diff_rx as AON and move > these AND gates into that block? > > https://github.com/lowRISC/opentitan/blob/17d2866a497d7bb0e891e32eddb99c943e3cbafb/hw/top_earlgrey/rtl/autogen/chip_earlgrey_asic.sv#L743-L754 > >   > You are receiving this because you were assigned. > Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub > <https://github.com/lowRISC/opentitan/issues/7802#issuecomment-901365407>, > or unsubscribe > <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAH2RSS7IFRAOBATN246MSTT5QBIFANCNFSM5CLLKSFA> > . > Triage notifications on the go with GitHub Mobile for iOS > <https://apps.apple.com/app/apple-store/id1477376905?ct=notification-email&mt=8&pt=524675> > or Android > <https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.github.android&utm_campaign=notification-email> > . >  msfschaffner: hm yes, that would work as well, but then we would have to make these intermodule signal outputs from the usb module. otherwise this messes with the DIO accounting in the topgen scripts... tjaychen: yes that's right. Sorry, i should clarify.  The usb aon wake module is actually inside pinmux, because usbdev itself is not always on. So we would make an intermodule connection directly from there to the usb rx differential receive i think.  On Wed, Aug 18, 2021 at 12:22 PM Michael Schaffner ***@***.***> wrote:  > hm yes, that would work as well, but then we would have to make these > intermodule signal outputs from the usb module. otherwise this messes with > the DIO accounting in the topgen scripts... > >   > You are receiving this because you were assigned. > Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub > <https://github.com/lowRISC/opentitan/issues/7802#issuecomment-901368964>, > or unsubscribe > <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAH2RSUDBRM35XA7B7KZGBDT5QB6FANCNFSM5CLLKSFA> > . > Triage notifications on the go with GitHub Mobile for iOS > <https://apps.apple.com/app/apple-store/id1477376905?ct=notification-email&mt=8&pt=524675> > or Android > <https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.github.android&utm_campaign=notification-email> > . >  msfschaffner: yeah that sounds good. let me take a look at this (I am in the process of getting pinmux D2 ready https://github.com/lowRISC/opentitan/pull/7721 and there are some other assertions that need to be cleaned up). msfschaffner: Actually, I found a simpler solution that does not require a lot of rewiring.  I.e., we can just remove the AND gates in this case - see #7802. msfschaffner: @arnonsha can you double check whether this is now solved (and close this issue if it does)? arnonsha: fixed in #7830. closing this issue
